title: Glorious Golden Middle Eastern Chicken Kebabs
description: A crowd-pleasing Middle Eastern dish featuring marinated chicken, barbeque sauce, and a sweet and tangy flavor profile, this recipe is sugar-free-friendly and perfect for outdoor gatherings.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the yogurt, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken to the bowl and mix until the chicken is well coated with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Thread the marinated chicken onto the soaked wooden skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with oil to prevent sticking and grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally.
After 8 minutes of grilling, brush the kebabs with the sugar-free barbeque sauce.
Continue grilling for an additional 2-4 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
